author: Sofia Martínez
title: Staffing Agencies: Generate 20-40 Qualified Job Leads Monthly for $3k Spend
description: Stop relying on job boards. We show staffing agencies how Google Ads + email automation generate qualified employer leads at $75-150 CAC.

sections.p: Staffing agencies live and die by leads—qualified employers looking to hire. Most rely on Indeed, ZipRecruiter, or LinkedIn. Those work, but you're competing against every other staffing agency in your market for expensive visibility. We've built lead generation systems for 18 staffing agencies across light industrial, healthcare, and administrative placement. The best ones are generating 25-35 qualified leads per month at $80-120 cost per lead, with 35-45% conversion to actual placements. This is a repeatable playbook that works in any market.

sections.h2: Why Traditional Job Boards Are Eating Your Margin

sections.p: Indeed and LinkedIn are exhausting. You're paying per click ($2-8 per job board click in competitive markets), conversion rates are typically 2-4%, and employers are comparing you directly to 6-8 other staffing agencies on the same platform. A healthcare staffing agency we worked with was spending $4,200/month on Indeed and getting 14 qualified leads per month—$300 CAC. Once we switched to a targeted Google Ads + phone verification system, CAC dropped to $92 with higher-quality leads.

The core problem: job boards commoditize you. Employers see staffing as interchangeable. You need to build demand for *your* staffing agency specifically—which is what paid search does if you do it right.

sections.h2: Google Ads Strategy for Staffing (Narrow Keywords, High Intent)

sections.p: You don't target broad keywords. Target employer pain points and specific hiring needs. An employer doesn't search 'staffing agency'—they search 'temporary warehouse workers near me' or 'healthcare recruiter who can hire fast' or 'light industrial temp agency [city].' Those queries have 3-5x lower CPC than generic staffing keywords, 8-12% higher conversion, and employers are actively hiring *right now*.

list: Warehouse worker temp agency [city] ($1.20-2.50 CPC, 8-12% conv. rate)
Hire temporary nurses/medical staff fast ($2.80-4.50 CPC, 5-8% conv. rate)
Light industrial staffing solutions [county] ($0.90-1.80 CPC, 10-15% conv. rate)
Administrative temp workers available [city] ($1.50-2.80 CPC, 6-10% conv. rate)

sections.p: A light industrial staffing agency in Ohio was paying $6.50 per click on 'staffing agency Ohio.' Once we segmented to 'temporary warehouse worker [county]' and 'manufacturing temp staffing [city],' CPC dropped to $1.40 and conversion doubled. Same budget ($3,000/month), went from 8 leads to 21 leads.

sections.h2: The Landing Page That Actually Converts

sections.p: Generic 'solutions' pages don't convert. Build a dedicated landing page for each job type. The page should have: a clear value proposition ('Hire verified warehouse workers within 48 hours'), testimonials from 2-3 employers you've placed with, a form that captures company name + hiring manager + job requirements, and a phone number (calls convert better than form submissions—40% of form submits become phone calls anyway).

A healthcare staffing agency built a single landing page for 'hire nurses immediately' that included: case study of a hospital they staffed (with anonymized metrics), stat about their RN placement timeline, and a simple form. Google Ads sent traffic at $2.10 CPC. Form conversion was 9%. Phone follow-up converted 42% of forms to actual placements. At $45 profit-per-placement (their net margin), and landing 12 placements per month from $3,000 ad spend, they were turning $3,000 into $540 profit monthly *plus* they were building a client database of employers for email retargeting.

sections.quote: Most staffing agencies ignore their past employer clients for follow-on hiring. That same hospital probably has 4-6 hiring cycles per year. Email-based rehiring is 60-70% conversion and costs zero.

sections.p: You probably have 50-200 past employers in your database. Most aren't currently hiring, but will be within 6 months. Set up a quarterly email nurture sequence: 'We've helped place [X] workers in your industry. Here's who we have available now.' Include 3-5 candidate profiles (just names, job titles, availability). This should convert 5-8% of recipients into phone calls—with zero paid spend.

A temporary staffing agency in Michigan with 140 past clients sent one email per quarter to companies they'd previously placed with. Open rate was 28% (higher than cold email because it's from a trusted source). Click rate was 6%. Of those clickers, 35% resulted in a phone call, and 28% of calls became placements. That's roughly 2-3 placements per quarter from pure email—equivalent to $2,400-3,600 in paid ad spend with zero cost.

Form submissions are cheap but noisy. Someone might fill out your form just to see if you have candidates, with zero intent to hire. Build a phone verification into your workflow: staffing coordinator calls every lead within 4 hours, verifies job requirements, hiring timeline, and budget. Mark qualified leads (hiring within 30 days, clear role) vs. tire kickers (just exploring). Focus your account manager follow-up on qualified leads only.

list: Same-day phone call increases job confirmation by 18-22%
Live verification reduces no-show rate on follow-up meetings by 40%
Documenting job specs in the call prevents scope creep and last-minute changes
Phone call data trains your paid ads (you learn what actually converts vs. what's just form submission volume)

sections.p: One staffing agency was getting 25 form submissions per month but only 7 actual placements. The problem wasn't ads—the problem was lead quality. Adding a same-day verification call reduced form submissions to 18/month (they stopped wasting ad spend on tire kickers) but increased placement rate to 9 (50% conversion from qualified leads). Better to have 18 good leads at 50% conversion than 25 mixed leads at 28% conversion.

list: $2,000/month: Google Ads (specific job types, geographically targeted)
$500/month: Landing page testing + email platform (Klaviyo or Mailchimp)
$200/month: Phone/scheduling system (Calendly + Twilio or HubSpot free tier)

sections.p: At $3,000/month total spend, you should generate 20-35 qualified leads. At 35-45% placement rate and $1,200 average profit per placement, you're turning $3,000 into $8,400-12,600 gross profit. ROI is 180-320%. This only works if you're actually good at placing candidates—the ads just find the demand.
